zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Simon of Marston St Lawrence, Northumberland de SaintElizabeth於1198年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾馬爾斯頓 聖 勞倫斯時，他的父親Simon IV de Saint Elizabeth33歲，母親Amicia31歲。他於1231年與Anne Lady Marston Northumberland de Balistarius在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾馬爾斯頓 聖 勞倫斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1250年在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德瓦可沃斯享年52歲。

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Marston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from one or other of the numerous places called Marston or Merston, in counties including Bedfordshire, Cheshire, Derbyshire, Gloucestershire, Isle of Wight, Kent, Northamptonshire, Oxfordshire, Shropshire, Staffordshire, Sussex, and Worcestershire. Most of the placenames derive from Old English mersc ‘marsh’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Marson
meanings: Mainly North American: transferred use of the mainly Scottish surname, which is of uncertain derivation. The first known bearer of the surname is a certain Robert de Carsan (or de Acarson), recorded in 1276 ; the ‘de’ suggests derivation from a place name, but no suitable place has been identified. Among Protestants in Northern Ireland, it is sometimes bestowed in honour of Edward Carson ( 1854–1935 ), the Dublin barrister and politician who was a violent opponent of Home Rule for Ireland. In America the popularity of the name may have been affected by the legendary Missouri frontiersman Kit Carson ( 1809–68 ).

variants.name: Maston
meanings: Of uncertain origin; a traditional name in aristocratic families of Languedoc, so perhaps an altered form of Gascon, denoting someone from Gascony in Languedoc.

nameMeanings.name: St
meanings: Cornish: A Cornish form of Just (Prouter Est, the priest of St. Just).

nameMeanings.name: Lawrence,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized spelling of   Laurence .  This is the usual spelling of the surname, and is now becoming increasingly common as a given name, especially in North America.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Simon of Marston St Lawrence, Northumberland de SaintElizabeth was born in 1198, in Marston St Lawrence, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Simon IV de Saint Elizabeth, was 33 and his mother, Amicia, was 31. He married Anne Lady Marston Northumberland de Balistarius in 1231, in Marston St Lawrence, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1250, in Warkworth, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52.

Il a épousé Anne Lady Marston Northumberland de Balistarius en 1231, à Marston St Lawrence, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1250, à Warkworth, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Simon of Marston St Lawrence, Northumberland de SaintElizabethが1198年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアマーストン スト ラウレンスで生まれた時，父Simon IV de Saint Elizabethは33歳，母Amiciaは31歳であった。1231年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアマーストン スト ラウレンスでAnne Lady Marston Northumberland de Balistariusと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1250年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドワークウオルスで52歳で死去した。

ko.Marston St Lawrence, Northumberland, Eng: 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 마스턴 스트 로렌스
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Simon of Marston St Lawrence, Northumberland de SaintElizabeth(이)가 1198년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 마스턴 스트 로렌스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Simon IV de Saint Elizabeth(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Amicia(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1231년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 마스턴 스트 로렌스에서 Anne Lady Marston Northumberland de Balistarius(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1250년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 워k워스에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Marston St Lawrence, Northumberland, Eng: марстон ст ларенс, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Simon of Marston St Lawrence, Northumberland de SaintElizabeth родился в 1198 г. в местности марстон ст ларенс, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Simon IV de Saint Elizabeth было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Amicia, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Lady Marston Northumberland de Balistarius в 1231 г. в местности марстон ст ларенс, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1250 г. в местности варкворт, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет.
